question: US × Iran ceasefire by April 7?
narrative_context: Iran rejected Trump's 15-point peace plan on March 25, presenting unrealistic counteroffer demanding Strait of Hormuz sovereignty and reparations (NPR, March 25 2026)
Israel struck IRGC targets and Isfahan facilities; Iran fired missiles at central Israel on March 27–28 (Al Jazeera live blog, April 1 2026)
Trump paused energy plant strikes until April 6 8pm EST, but Israeli officials assess ceasefire odds as 'low' despite extended pause (WaPo/PBS, April 1 2026)
Only 11 days to close; resolution requires mutual public confirmation of ceasefire AND 14 consecutive zero-action days starting by April 1—already impossible (Polymarket, March 30 2026)
thesis: Market prices ceasefire at 14%, but it's closer to 3%. Iran has publicly rejected peace talks, both sides are actively escalating (missiles, airstrikes), and the close date (April 7) leaves only 11 days for both sides to reverse course AND execute 14 consecutive zero-action days. Israeli officials explicitly assess odds as low. This is a near-certain NO.
x_thread: Polymarket says Iran-US ceasefire by April 7 is 14%. We think 3%. Here's why the market is badly wrong.
Iran just rejected Trump's 15-point peace plan. Issued their own 5-point counteroffer: Strait sovereignty + reparations. Both non-starters for the US.
Last 48h: Israel struck IRGC + Isfahan; Iran fired missiles at Israel. Trump paused energy strikes until Apr 6—but Israeli military assess ceasefire odds as LOW.
Only 11 days left. Resolution requires mutual public ceasefire + 14 straight days of zero action. Already mathematically impossible. Market @ 14% is fantasy. Check: https://dub.sh/pb-x
telegram_post:
🔴 **Iran-US Ceasefire by April 7? Market: 14% | Reality: ~3%**

Polymarket is deeply mispriced on this one.

**The thesis:**
• Iran publicly rejected peace talks and Trump's plan (March 25)
• Active escalation: Israel struck IRGC + Isfahan; Iran fired missiles (March 27–28)
• Trump paused energy strikes until April 6, but Israeli officials say ceasefire odds are LOW
• Only 11 days to close; resolution requires 14 straight zero-action days starting by April 1—already impossible

**Why the market is wrong:** 14% prices in a dramatic reversal + simultaneous diplomatic breakthrough + perfect implementation. The military reality on the ground is escalation, not de-escalation. This is a near-certain NO.
